scenario: A normal OpenClaw/LCO operator asks what to do next and the agent chooses the compact public facade before dropping into expert or debug tools.
expected_public_safe_evidence: manifest tool declarations include metadata.tier for every loo_* tool
exactly 6-8 public_facade tools are present and ordered by operatorPathRank
agent guidance names the compact public facade before workflow_detail or proof_debug tools
workflow_detail, proof_debug, and internal_low_level tools remain declared
safety.mode, requiresApproval, and mutationClasses are unchanged by facade metadata
LOO/LCO naming policy keeps loo_ canonical and routes alias work to #434
pass_criteria: Given the full tool list, the agent starts with loo_prepared_inbox or another public_facade tool rather than scanning every declared tool as a peer.
The agent uses loo_describe_ref and loo_expand_query for bounded detail instead of opening raw transcripts or low-level stores.
The agent uses loo_codex_control_dry_run before any live control and treats loo_codex_resume_thread as approval-gated.
The agent enters workflow_detail, proof_debug, or internal_low_level tiers only when the facade returns a specific missing evidence, proof, setup, or recovery reason.
The manifest snapshot test fails if a new tool lacks one of public_facade, workflow_detail, proof_debug, or internal_low_level.
Docs and skill prompts keep expert/debug tools available and explicit instead of deleting, hiding, or over-consolidating them.
The naming policy preserves loo_* as the canonical tool prefix and does not invent broad lco_* aliases outside #434.
fail_criteria: Agent treats all declared loo_* tools as equally recommended first steps.
Agent starts with proof_debug or internal_low_level tools for ordinary operator questions without a stated blocker.
A new tool can be added without choosing a tier.
Facade docs imply expert/debug tools were removed or hidden.
Facade metadata weakens approval, mutation-class, or public-safety boundaries.
Docs rename tools to lco_* without an explicit #434 compatibility plan.
known_gaps: This scorecard proves routing guidance and manifest classification, not real agent behavior from a live OpenClaw gateway transcript.
Future alias implementation, if any, belongs with #434 and needs separate compatibility tests.
next_action: Run the manifest snapshot test and focused scorecard tests whenever a new loo_* tool is added or the public facade changes.
proof_boundary: This proves public-safe facade metadata, docs, skill prompts, and advisory scorecard coverage for choosing a compact operator path. It does not remove tools, prove live OpenClaw gateway behavior, approve live Codex control, mutate a GUI, publish npm, create a GitHub Release, or implement broad lco_* aliases.
